ogTitle: Bung Bong vs Bungal: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bung Bong and Bungal.
heading: Bung Bong vs Bungal — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bung Bong, Victoria and Bungal, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bung Bong has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Bungal (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bung Bong or Bungal safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bung Bong has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Bungal scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bung Bong scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Bungal scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bung Bong or Bungal?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bung Bong has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
